items.front: What information must ATC determine when an aircraft plans to dump fuel?
items.back: Determine the route and altitude to be flown and the weather conditions in which the dumping will occur.

items.front: When may ATC request a different route for fuel dumping?
items.back: An aircraft dumping fuel may be asked to fly a different route unless the dumping is for emergency reasons.

items.front: What altitude must be assigned when an aircraft dumps fuel in IFR conditions?
items.back: At least 2,000 feet above the highest obstacle within 5 miles of the route or pattern being flown.

items.front: How are VFR radar-identified aircraft separated from an aircraft dumping fuel?
items.back: Separate them by 5 miles and in accordance with the applicable radar procedures.

items.front: How long are fuel-dumping advisories broadcast and how often?
items.back: Facilities concerned broadcast a fuel-dumping advisory every 3 minutes until the operation stops, then broadcast a termination advisory.
